skills.skill: Proportionality - generalize that the ratio of corresponding sides of similar shapes are proportional, including a shape and its dilation
skills.prompt: This topic covers Proportionality - generalize that the ratio of corresponding sides of similar shapes are proportional, including a shape and its dilation for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(A)). This aligns with TEKS standard 8.3(A). Students at this level should develop proficiency in this skill, building on prior knowledge and connecting to grade-level expectations. Key concepts include: understanding the underlying mathematical principles, applying appropriate procedures and strategies, solving both computational and contextual problems, and justifying solutions using mathematical reasoning. Students should represent the concept using multiple representations (numerical, graphical, verbal, algebraic) and make connections to real-world applications. Vocabulary and notation specific to this topic should be used precisely. Common misconceptions should be addressed through careful instruction and varied practice. Problems and explanations should be appropriate for 13-14 year olds at the Grade 8 level, progressing from concrete to abstract understanding.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Similar and congruent figures
skills.prompt: This topic covers similar and congruent figures for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(A)). Students distinguish between similar figures (same shape, proportional sides) and congruent figures (same shape and size). Key concepts include: corresponding sides and angles, proportional relationships in similar figures, congruence as a special case of similarity with scale factor 1, and transformations that produce each type. Vocabulary: similar, congruent, corresponding sides, corresponding angles, proportional, scale factor, transformation. Common misconceptions: confusing similar with congruent and thinking similarity requires equal sides. Practice should include identification and comparison appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Identify similar triangles
skills.prompt: This topic covers identifying similar triangles for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(A)). Students determine whether two triangles are similar by checking proportional sides or equal angles. Key concepts include: AA (angle-angle) similarity criterion, SSS similarity (all corresponding sides proportional), SAS similarity, and computing scale factors. Vocabulary: similar triangles, AA criterion, corresponding sides, corresponding angles, scale factor, proportional. Common misconceptions: requiring all three angles to be checked when two suffice and confusing similarity with congruence. Practice should include identification from given measurements appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Side lengths and angle measures of similar figures
skills.prompt: This topic covers finding side lengths and angle measures of similar figures for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(A)). Students use proportional relationships to find unknown measurements in similar figures. Key concepts include: setting up proportions with corresponding sides, understanding that corresponding angles are equal in similar figures, and computing scale factors. Vocabulary: similar figures, corresponding, proportional, scale factor, angle, side length, ratio. Common misconceptions: setting up proportions with non-corresponding sides, not recognizing which sides correspond, and thinking angles change. Practice should include finding missing measurements appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Similar solids
skills.prompt: This topic covers similar solids for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(A)). Students extend similarity concepts to three-dimensional figures. Key concepts include: corresponding dimensions are proportional, scale factor applies to all linear dimensions, surface area scales by k^2, volume scales by k^3, and setting up proportions. Vocabulary: similar solids, scale factor, corresponding, proportional, surface area ratio, volume ratio, cube, square. Common misconceptions: applying the linear scale factor directly to surface area or volume, and not understanding the squared and cubed relationships. Practice should include 3D similarity problems appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - compare and contrast the attributes of a shape and its dilation(s) on a coordinate plane
skills.prompt: This topic covers comparing attributes of a shape and its dilation for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(B)). Students analyze how dilation changes a figure on a coordinate plane. Key concepts include: dilated figures are similar to the original, side lengths change by the scale factor, angles remain the same, area changes by the square of the scale factor, and the center of dilation affects position. Vocabulary: dilation, scale factor, similar, coordinate plane, center of dilation, attribute, preserve, proportional. Common misconceptions: thinking angles change during dilation and confusing enlargement with reduction. Practice should include coordinate plane dilations appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Dilations: find the scale factor
skills.prompt: This topic covers finding the scale factor for dilations for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(B)). Students determine the scale factor by comparing corresponding measurements of a figure and its dilation. Key concepts include: scale factor = image length / original length, scale factor > 1 means enlargement, 0 < scale factor < 1 means reduction, and using coordinates to find scale factors. Vocabulary: dilation, scale factor, image, pre-image, enlargement, reduction, corresponding, ratio. Common misconceptions: dividing in the wrong order and confusing enlargement with reduction. Practice should include coordinate and non-coordinate problems appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - use an algebraic representation to explain the effect of a given positive rational scale factor applied to two-dimensional figures on a coordinate plane with the origin as the center of dilation
skills.prompt: This topic covers algebraic representations of dilations for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(C)). Students use coordinate rules (x, y) -> (kx, ky) to describe dilations with the origin as center. Key concepts include: applying the scale factor k to both coordinates, understanding k > 1 enlarges and 0 < k < 1 reduces, the effect on the figure's position relative to the origin, and connecting the algebraic rule to the geometric transformation. Vocabulary: dilation, scale factor, algebraic representation, coordinate rule, origin, center of dilation, image. Common misconceptions: not applying k to both coordinates and not understanding the origin as center. Practice should include applying algebraic rules appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Dilations: graph the image
skills.prompt: This topic covers graphing dilated images for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(C)). Students graph the image of a figure after dilation on a coordinate plane. Key concepts include: multiplying each coordinate by the scale factor, plotting the new vertices, connecting to form the image, and verifying similarity. Vocabulary: dilation, scale factor, image, pre-image, coordinate plane, vertex, graph. Common misconceptions: not applying the scale factor to both x and y, and errors with fractional scale factors. Practice should include graphing on coordinate planes appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Dilations: find the coordinates
skills.prompt: This topic covers finding coordinates after dilation for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(C)). Students calculate the coordinates of a figure's image after dilation using (x, y) -> (kx, ky). Key concepts include: multiplying both coordinates by the scale factor, working with integer and fractional scale factors, and identifying the center of dilation. Vocabulary: dilation, scale factor, coordinates, image, pre-image, origin, center of dilation. Common misconceptions: applying the scale factor to only one coordinate and arithmetic errors with fractions. Practice should include various scale factors appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Checkpoint: Dilations and similarity
skills.prompt: This topic is a checkpoint assessment on dilations and similarity for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.3(A)). Students demonstrate mastery of identifying similar figures, finding scale factors, performing dilations on coordinate planes, and understanding the relationship between dilations and similarity. Key concepts: proportional sides, equal angles, scale factor calculations, coordinate transformations, and the algebraic rule (x,y) -> (kx, ky). Vocabulary: dilation, similarity, scale factor, corresponding, proportional. Assessment should cover identification, computation, and graphing appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - use similar right triangles to develop an understanding that slope, m, given as the rate comparing the change in y-values to the change in x-values, (y2 - y1)/ (x2 - x1), is the same for any two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) on the same line
skills.prompt: This topic covers using similar right triangles to understand slope for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(A)). Students discover that the slope between any two points on a line is the same by forming right triangles and showing they are similar. Key concepts include: slope as rise/run or (y2-y1)/(x2-x1), similar triangles formed by any two points on the same line have equal ratios of vertical to horizontal change, and slope is constant for a line. Vocabulary: slope, similar triangles, rise, run, ratio, constant, linear, change in y, change in x. Common misconceptions: thinking slope can change along a line and confusing the order of subtraction. Practice should include geometric slope exploration appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - graph proportional relationships, interpreting the unit rate as the slope of the line that models the relationship
skills.prompt: This topic covers graphing proportional relationships and interpreting slope as unit rate for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(B)). Students graph proportional relationships and understand that the slope of the line equals the unit rate (constant of proportionality). Key concepts include: proportional graphs pass through the origin, slope = k in y = kx, reading the unit rate from the graph at x = 1, and comparing slopes of different proportional relationships. Vocabulary: proportional relationship, slope, unit rate, constant of proportionality, origin, graph, y = kx. Common misconceptions: confusing slope with y-intercept and not recognizing that proportional graphs must pass through (0,0). Practice should include graphing and interpretation appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Find the constant of proportionality from a graph
skills.prompt: This topic covers finding the constant of proportionality from a graph for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(B)). Students determine k in y = kx by reading a point from the graph and computing y/x. Key concepts include: choosing clear points, computing k = y/x, understanding k as slope through the origin, and using the unit rate point (1, k). Vocabulary: constant of proportionality, graph, slope, origin, unit rate, y = kx. Common misconceptions: reading coordinates incorrectly, using x/y, and selecting points not on the line. Practice should include graphs with various scales appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Graph proportional relationships and find the slope
skills.prompt: This topic covers graphing proportional relationships and finding slope for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(B)). Students plot proportional data and determine the slope of the resulting line. Key concepts include: plotting ordered pairs, drawing the line through origin, calculating slope as rise/run or y/x, and interpreting slope as the unit rate. Vocabulary: proportional, graph, slope, rise, run, unit rate, origin, coordinate plane. Common misconceptions: not passing the line through the origin and errors in slope calculation. Practice should include graphing and slope computation appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - use data from a table or graph to determine the rate of change or slope and y-intercept in mathematical and real-world problems
skills.prompt: This topic covers determining slope and y-intercept from tables and graphs for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students extract rate of change (slope) and initial value (y-intercept) from data representations. Key concepts include: slope from a table as change in y / change in x between rows, slope from a graph as rise/run, y-intercept as the value when x = 0, and interpreting both in context. Vocabulary: slope, y-intercept, rate of change, initial value, table, graph, rise, run, linear. Common misconceptions: using only one data point for slope, confusing x-intercept with y-intercept, and not interpreting in context. Practice should include tables and graphs from real-world problems appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Find the constant of proportionality from a table
skills.prompt: This topic covers finding the constant of proportionality from a table for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students calculate k in y = kx by dividing y-values by corresponding x-values. Key concepts include: computing y/x for each row, verifying consistency, understanding k as the unit rate, and writing the equation. Vocabulary: constant of proportionality, table, ratio, y = kx, unit rate. Common misconceptions: computing x/y instead of y/x, not checking all rows, and confusing additive with multiplicative patterns. Practice should include real-world tables appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Find the constant of proportionality from a graph
skills.prompt: This topic covers finding the constant of proportionality from a graph for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students determine k in y = kx by reading a point from the graph and computing y/x. Key concepts include: choosing clear points, computing k = y/x, understanding k as slope through the origin, and using the unit rate point (1, k). Vocabulary: constant of proportionality, graph, slope, origin, unit rate, y = kx. Common misconceptions: reading coordinates incorrectly, using x/y, and selecting points not on the line. Practice should include graphs with various scales appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Find the slope from a graph
skills.prompt: This topic covers finding slope from a graph for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students calculate the slope of a line by identifying two points and computing rise over run. Key concepts include: choosing two clear points, calculating vertical change (rise) and horizontal change (run), dividing rise by run, and determining if slope is positive, negative, zero, or undefined. Vocabulary: slope, rise, run, graph, positive slope, negative slope, zero slope, undefined slope. Common misconceptions: computing run/rise instead of rise/run, not accounting for negative slopes, and reading coordinates incorrectly. Practice should include lines with various slopes appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Find the slope from a table
skills.prompt: This topic covers finding slope from a table for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students calculate slope by finding the ratio of change in y-values to change in x-values between table entries. Key concepts include: selecting any two rows, computing (y2-y1)/(x2-x1), verifying with multiple pairs for linear data, and interpreting the slope in context. Vocabulary: slope, table, rate of change, change in y, change in x, ratio, linear. Common misconceptions: subtracting in inconsistent order, only checking one pair, and confusing slope with y-values. Practice should include tables from real-world contexts appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Rate of change of a linear function: tables
skills.prompt: This topic covers rate of change of linear functions from tables for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students determine whether a table represents a linear function by checking for a constant rate of change. Key concepts include: computing change in y / change in x for consecutive entries, verifying constancy, interpreting the rate in context, and connecting to the slope of the line. Vocabulary: rate of change, linear function, constant, table, slope, proportional. Common misconceptions: assuming all tables are linear and only checking one pair of rows. Practice should include identifying linear and non-linear tables appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Rate of change of a linear function: graphs
skills.prompt: This topic covers rate of change of linear functions from graphs for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students identify the rate of change from graphs and determine if it is constant (linear). Key concepts include: constant rate of change produces a straight line, computing slope between points, comparing rates at different parts of the graph, and connecting to the equation. Vocabulary: rate of change, linear function, constant, graph, slope, straight line. Common misconceptions: confusing steepness with constant rate and assuming curved graphs have constant rates. Practice should include varied graph analysis appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Interpret the slope and y-intercept of a linear function
skills.prompt: This topic covers interpreting slope and y-intercept in context for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students explain the real-world meaning of slope (rate of change) and y-intercept (initial value) of linear functions. Key concepts include: slope as how fast y changes per unit of x, y-intercept as the starting value when x = 0, and connecting both to the equation y = mx + b. Vocabulary: slope, y-intercept, rate of change, initial value, linear function, interpret, context. Common misconceptions: reversing the meanings of slope and y-intercept and not connecting to real-world context. Practice should include contextual interpretation problems appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Write a linear function from a table
skills.prompt: This topic covers writing a linear function from a table for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). Students determine the equation of a linear function from table data. Key concepts include: finding slope from the table, identifying the y-intercept (value when x=0), and writing the equation in y = mx + b form. Vocabulary: linear function, slope, y-intercept, table, equation, y = mx + b, rate of change. Common misconceptions: errors in computing slope, not finding the y-intercept correctly when x=0 is not in the table, and confusing slope with y-intercept. Practice should include tables from real-world contexts appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Checkpoint: Interpret slopes and y-intercepts
skills.prompt: This topic covers Proportionality - Checkpoint: Interpret slopes and y-intercepts for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.4(C)). This aligns with TEKS standard 8.4(C). Students at this level should develop proficiency in this skill, building on prior knowledge and connecting to grade-level expectations. Key concepts include: understanding the underlying mathematical principles, applying appropriate procedures and strategies, solving both computational and contextual problems, and justifying solutions using mathematical reasoning. Students should represent the concept using multiple representations (numerical, graphical, verbal, algebraic) and make connections to real-world applications. Vocabulary and notation specific to this topic should be used precisely. Common misconceptions should be addressed through careful instruction and varied practice. Problems and explanations should be appropriate for 13-14 year olds at the Grade 8 level, progressing from concrete to abstract understanding.

This aligns with TEKS standard 8.5(A). Students at this level should develop proficiency in this skill, building on prior knowledge and connecting to grade-level expectations. Students should represent the concept using multiple representations (numerical, graphical, verbal, algebraic) and make connections to real-world applications. Vocabulary and notation specific to this topic should be used precisely. Common misconceptions should be addressed through careful instruction and varied practice. skills.skill: Proportionality - Proportional relationships: complete a table and make a graph
skills.prompt: This topic covers Proportionality - Proportional relationships: complete a table and make a graph for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(A)). This aligns with TEKS standard 8.5(A). Students at this level should develop proficiency in this skill, building on prior knowledge and connecting to grade-level expectations. skills.skill: Proportionality - Write equations for proportional relationships from tables
skills.prompt: This topic covers writing equations for proportional relationships from tables for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(A)). Students analyze tables to identify proportional relationships and write equations in y = kx form. Key concepts include: calculating k by dividing y by x for each row, verifying consistency, and writing the equation. Vocabulary: proportional relationship, constant of proportionality, ratio, table, equation, y = kx. Common misconceptions: confusing additive and multiplicative relationships, not checking all rows, and computing x/y instead of y/x. Practice should include tables from real-world contexts appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Write equations for proportional relationships from graphs
skills.prompt: This topic covers writing equations for proportional relationships from graphs for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(A)). Students read graphs and write equations in y = kx form. Key concepts include: verifying the graph passes through the origin, finding k by choosing a point and computing y/x, and writing the equation. Vocabulary: proportional relationship, constant of proportionality, graph, origin, slope, y = kx. Common misconceptions: reading coordinates incorrectly and using x/y instead of y/x. Practice should include various graph interpretations appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Graph proportional relationships and find the slope
skills.prompt: This topic covers Proportionality - Graph proportional relationships and find the slope for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(A)). This aligns with TEKS standard 8.5(A). Students at this level should develop proficiency in this skill, building on prior knowledge and connecting to grade-level expectations. skills.skill: Proportionality - Interpret graphs of proportional relationships
skills.prompt: This topic covers interpreting graphs of proportional relationships for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(A)). Students analyze graphs to extract meaning in context. Key concepts include: identifying the constant of proportionality, understanding specific points (including the unit rate at x=1), comparing proportional relationships on the same graph, and connecting to real-world meaning. Vocabulary: proportional, graph, constant of proportionality, unit rate, origin, slope. Common misconceptions: misreading scale, confusing axis meanings, and not connecting to real-world context. Practice should include contextual graph interpretation appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Interpret graphs of proportional relationships
skills.prompt: This topic covers interpreting graphs of proportional relationships for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(E)). Students analyze graphs to extract meaning in context. Key concepts include: identifying the constant of proportionality, understanding specific points (including the unit rate at x=1), comparing proportional relationships on the same graph, and connecting to real-world meaning. Vocabulary: proportional, graph, constant of proportionality, unit rate, origin, slope. Common misconceptions: misreading scale, confusing axis meanings, and not connecting to real-world context. Practice should include contextual graph interpretation appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Write and solve equations for proportional relationships
skills.prompt: This topic covers writing and solving equations for proportional relationships for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(E)). Students write equations in y = kx form and solve for unknown values. Key concepts include: determining k from given information, writing the equation, substituting to find unknowns, and interpreting solutions. Vocabulary: proportional relationship, constant of proportionality, equation, y = kx, solve, substitute. Common misconceptions: using incorrect k, confusing which variable to solve for, and not interpreting solutions in context. Practice should include real-world proportional scenarios appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - distinguish between proportional and non-proportional situations using tables, graphs, and equations in the form y = kx or y = mx + b, where b ≠ 0

This aligns with TEKS standard 8.5(F). Students at this level should develop proficiency in this skill, building on prior knowledge and connecting to grade-level expectations. Key concepts include: understanding the underlying mathematical principles, applying appropriate procedures and strategies, solving both computational and contextual problems, and justifying solutions using mathematical reasoning. Students should represent the concept using multiple representations (numerical, graphical, verbal, algebraic) and make connections to real-world applications. skills.skill: Proportionality - Identify proportional relationships by graphing
skills.prompt: This topic covers identifying proportional relationships by graphing for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(F)). Students determine whether a relationship is proportional by examining its graph. Key concepts include: proportional relationships produce straight lines through the origin (0,0), non-proportional linear relationships have y-intercepts other than zero, and recognizing proportionality from plotted points. Vocabulary: proportional, graph, origin, straight line, linear, y-intercept, coordinate plane. Common misconceptions: thinking any straight line is proportional (must pass through origin) and confusing linear with proportional. Practice should include graphing data sets appropriate for 13-14 year olds.

skills.skill: Proportionality - Identify proportional relationships from graphs and equations
skills.prompt: This topic covers identifying proportional relationships from graphs and equations for Grade 8 students (TEKS 8.5(F)). Students determine whether given graphs or equations represent proportional relationships. Key criteria: graphs must be straight lines through the origin; equations must be y = kx with no added constant. Key concepts include: distinguishing y = kx from y = mx + b where b is not 0. Vocabulary: proportional, equation, graph, origin, y = kx, y-intercept, linear. Common misconceptions: thinking y = 2x + 1 is proportional and not checking for the origin. Practice should include classifying multiple representations appropriate for 13-14 year olds.
